PersonaSimulationSection.title: AI Personas from Deep Interviews
PersonaSimulationSection.description: Advanced AI Personas that simulate authentic human cognition. Whether using our extensive library or creating private personas from your own interview data, these agents maintain consistent personalities and behavioral frameworks to provide realistic insights.
PersonaSimulationSection.technologyQuote: Our deep interview AI Personas are a form of high-precision consumer simulation technology. You can also apply this same powerful technology to your own interview transcripts to create secure, proprietary AI Personas for your exclusive use.
PersonaSimulationSection.technologyDescription: These agents maintain consistent cognitive patterns, emotional responses, and decision-making frameworks that enable authentic research conversations - achieving 85% human-like accuracy in behavioral simulation.
PersonaSimulationSection.learnMoreLink: Learn more about AI Personas from Deep Interviews
PersonaSimulationSection.accuracyDescription: Human-like behavioral consistency in AI persona responses
PersonaSimulationSection.differentiatorDescription: Unlike traditional survey data or statistical models, our AI Personas are sophisticated behavioral simulations that maintain consistent personality traits and decision-making logic in new contexts.

help.title: Questions?
help.description: Chat with our AI sage for instant answers

hero.body: Most AI is built to do tasks for people. We believe in a second form — AI that understands the subjective world of humans.

twoWorlds.quote: We do not react to reality, but to the models of reality in our heads.
twoWorlds.objectiveDesc: The domain of traditional AI — automating tasks, processing data, executing workflows.
twoWorlds.subjectiveDesc: Motivations, preferences, fears, and value hierarchies — understanding these is the key to solving business and social problems.

twoAgents.title: Simulate × Research
twoAgents.body: One AI simulates people. Another researches them — both real and virtual.

simulator.description: Through the Subjective World Model, simulates a real user — not a statistical average.

consumer2c.label: Consumer
consumer2c.description: Simulates everyday consumers and their purchase behavior.

professional2b.label: B2B Buyer
professional2b.description: Simulates business buyers in professional procurement scenarios.

expert.label: Industry Expert
expert.description: Simulates domain experts with industry know-how.

researcher.description: Asks questions, guides, and analyzes — works with both real and simulated users.

mockup.suppQ1: What drives your preference for premium brands?
mockup.suppQ2: How do peer recommendations influence decisions?
mockup.modQ: What does 'value for money' mean to each of you?
mockup.reply1: For me it's durability — not just the price tag.
mockup.reply2: I look at total cost — maintenance, accessories...
mockup.modSummary: Two distinct value frameworks emerged across the group.
mockup.insightText: Price sensitivity correlates inversely with brand loyalty across 78% of participants.

worldModel.title: Subjective World Model
worldModel.body: Trained on 1M real human data samples to simulate the subjective structure of specific individuals. Core hypothesis: human behavior is driven not by knowledge, but by the structure of one's subjective world.
worldModel.datasetBody: We define the subjective world not as a single semantic distribution, but as a hierarchical system driven by four structural layers.

expression.label: Expression
expression.question: How people express themselves
expression.description: Linguistic style, emotional distribution, value orientation, and identity signals.

story.label: Story
story.question: How people explain themselves
story.description: Motivational logic, causal attribution patterns, and self-concept structure.

cognition.label: Cognition
cognition.question: How people make decisions
cognition.sources: Behavioral judgment surveys, psychological paradigm tests
cognition.description: Risk preferences, cognitive biases, value hierarchies, and probabilistic reasoning.

behavior.label: Behavior
behavior.question: How people actually act
behavior.sources: Economic game experiments, real transaction behaviors
behavior.description: Calibrates how expression and judgment translate into real-world actions under practical constraints.

worldModel.crossTitle: What People Say vs. What They Actually Do
worldModel.crossBody: The most valuable insight lives in the distance between expression and behavior.

social.does: 62% of purchases follow social exposure within 48h.

worldModel.matrixNote: Expression↔Behavior gap is the largest signal — expressions serve identity performance more than self-disclosure.

consumerInsight.question: Understand Gen-Z skincare consumer core needs and purchase decision paths
consumerInsight.planStep1: Segment target consumers by behavior patterns
consumerInsight.planStep4: Conduct deep interviews with each segment
consumerInsight.scoutPost2: Why I switched to clean beauty
consumerInsight.reportFinding: "Clean beauty" surging with Gen-Z, but price sensitivity rising — opportunity for affordable-clean positioning with transparent ingredients.

conceptTesting.question: Test our new clean beauty concept with Gen-Z consumers — focus on positioning and price sensitivity
conceptTesting.chatUserMsg: Analyze the focus group results for our clean beauty concept
conceptTesting.chatAiReply: Focus group completed. 4/6 positive on concept, but price resistance at $35. Running scoring analysis now...
conceptTesting.discQ: How do you feel about a clean beauty product priced at $35 that uses only plant-based ingredients?
conceptTesting.discReply1: I love the plant-based angle, but $35 feels steep for something unproven. I'd try it at $25.
conceptTesting.discReply2: The ingredients list matters more than the label. Show me the science, not just the branding.
conceptTesting.discSummary: 4/6 positive on concept, but price resistance at $35. Ingredient transparency valued over marketing claims.
conceptTesting.reportFinding: Concept resonates with target audience, but $35 price point triggers hesitation. Recommend launch at $28 with science-backed proof points.

productRnD.question: Scout emerging product trends, generate new concept directions, and validate which route feels strongest for our brand.
productRnD.scoutPost1: This matcha protein bar changed my life
productRnD.scoutPost2: Why texture matters more than flavor
productRnD.scoutPost3: The snack I can't stop buying
productRnD.inspirationUserMsg: Cross-domain inspiration: what products share our health-conscious millennial audience?
productRnD.inspirationAiReply: Inspiration validated: artisanal tea culture (shared audience). Innovation: Longjing tea cracker × clean protein — familiar comfort format, new value dimension...
productRnD.reportFinding: Largest opportunity in convenient, high-protein snack bites with taste parity. Concept: artisanal tea-infused protein crackers — a familiar comfort format meets clean protein for health-conscious millennials.

uxVoc.question: Analyze e-commerce checkout experience pain points and Voice of Customer feedback
uxVoc.chatUserMsg: Synthesize the interview findings into a journey map
uxVoc.chatAiReply: 4/5 flagged hidden shipping at checkout as the primary pain point. Mapping the full customer journey now...
uxVoc.interviewQ: Walk me through your last purchase — what almost stopped you from completing it?
uxVoc.interviewA: The shipping cost. It showed up at the very end, felt like a trap. I almost abandoned the cart.
uxVoc.reportFinding: Hidden shipping at checkout is #1 pain point (4/5 flagged). Recommend: include shipping in price or show cost early in the journey.

salesTraining.selectDesc: Choose a persona to practice your sales pitch with
salesTraining.interviewQ: We're looking for an AI research platform that can handle enterprise-scale consumer studies. What can you tell me about your system's capabilities?
salesTraining.interviewA: Interesting, but I need specifics — how does your pricing model work for large-scale deployments? We run 50+ studies per quarter.
salesTraining.strength1: Strong product knowledge demonstrated with specific use cases
salesTraining.strength2: Good active listening — picked up on budget concerns early
salesTraining.improve1: Missed opportunity to quantify ROI — CTO needs hard numbers
salesTraining.improve2: Should have addressed enterprise security concerns proactively
salesTraining.strategy: For technical buyers like CTOs: lead with architecture and data security, quantify time-to-value, and prepare competitive benchmarks.

pricingAttribution.question: Research willingness-to-pay and optimal pricing architecture for our 3-tier SaaS model: Free / Pro $49 / Enterprise $99
pricingAttribution.discQ: At what price point would you consider upgrading from Free to Pro? What features would justify the jump?
pricingAttribution.discReply1: $49 feels right for the features. But the Enterprise at $99 needs clearer differentiation — what do I get that Pro doesn't have?
pricingAttribution.discReply2: Free tier is too limited — I'd churn before upgrading. Make the free tier sticky enough to build habits, then the upgrade sells itself.
pricingAttribution.discSummary: Consensus: $49 Pro is well-positioned. Enterprise needs ROI proof and clear feature differentiation. Free tier needs more value to drive conversion.
pricingAttribution.reportFinding: Mid-tier ($49) has strongest conversion. Enterprise tier needs ROI proof — abstract value claims fail with technical buyers. Recommend tiered pricing with usage-based Enterprise.

academicResearch.question: Simulate urban family responses to new parental leave policy across 200 archetypes in Tier 1-3 cities
academicResearch.chatUserMsg: Simulate parental leave policy impact across 200 archetypes
academicResearch.chatAiReply: Strongest positive response in Tier-2 cities (61%). Urban dual-income families remain skeptical about job security impact...
academicResearch.reportFinding: Overall +12% birth intention. Tier-2 most responsive. Urban dual-income needs additional childcare subsidy to move the needle.

investmentPrediction.question: Q4 Tech Market multi-source prediction — analyst reports, social sentiment, and expert panels
investmentPrediction.discQ: What's your outlook for Q4 tech sector performance, considering current macro conditions and regulatory uncertainty?
investmentPrediction.discReply1: Cautiously bullish. AI spending is real and accelerating, but valuations are stretched. Expect rotation from mega-caps to mid-cap AI infrastructure plays.
investmentPrediction.discReply2: The regulatory risk is being underpriced. Watch for EU AI Act implementation and potential US executive orders. Could trigger 5-8% correction.
investmentPrediction.discSummary: 3/4 sources cautiously bullish. Key risk: regulatory uncertainty. Strongest conviction in AI infrastructure sub-sector.
investmentPrediction.signalTrending: Multi-source: cautiously bullish consensus, AI infrastructure favored
investmentPrediction.reportFinding: Q4 cautiously bullish. Key risk: regulatory uncertainty. 3/4 sources agree. Strongest conviction in AI infrastructure and mid-cap rotation.

dataAssets.title: Assets of the Subjective World

persona.description: Built on real behavioral data and social science models. Each Persona maintains consistent personality, cognitive biases, and decision frameworks. Upload your own interview data to build a private Persona library.

panel.title: AI Panel
panel.description: Real respondents provide baseline calibration. AI samples provide scale. Real-time expandable samples no traditional panel company can offer.

solutions.title: Who Uses It. What It Changes.
solutions.body: Breadth matters, but investors need operational proof. These deployments show Atypica cutting research cycles, removing recruiting bottlenecks, simulating policy outcomes at scale, and giving both humans and agents a callable layer of subjective intelligence.
solutions.coverageTitle: Across GTM, product, research, and finance
solutions.coverageBody: People can use the product directly, but the deeper wedge is that agents can call the same system as research infrastructure.
solutions.investorNoteBody: Human workflows now. Agent-native infrastructure next.

creators.title: Creators
creators.desc: Discover what will go viral and optimize content strategy.

influencers.title: Influencers
influencers.desc: Turn influence into sustainable income with data-driven insights.

marketers.title: Marketers
marketers.desc: Validate campaigns with real consumer insights before launch.

startupOwners.title: Startup Owners
startupOwners.desc: Kill bad ideas early and validate before spending.

consultants.title: Consultants
consultants.desc: Back recommendations with real consumer research.

researcher.title: Researcher
researcher.desc: AI-augmented social science across interviews, simulations, and ethnography.

investor.title: Investor
investor.desc: Multi-perspective simulation and continuous signal tracking for event prediction.

food.indicator: Campaign concept testing collapsed from months into days.
food.quote: We went from months of concept testing to days, and the AI-selected concept outperformed our control by 23%.
food.challenge: A Lunar New Year campaign needed dozens of localized ideas across multiple markets, but classic concept testing was too slow.
food.solution: The team used AI consumers to co-create and filter chocolate concepts, then cross-checked the signal against real interviews.
food.result: Concept volume increased, weak ideas were filtered early, and the final launch concept beat the control in market validation.

tools.indicator: Always-on prosumer feedback without recruiting spend or scheduling drag.
tools.quote: Professional users are expensive and hard to recruit. Now we have an always-on virtual panel that gives real-time feedback on our CAD prototypes.
tools.challenge: Prosumer testers were expensive, availability was inconsistent, and early prototype reviews stalled waiting for field recruitment.
tools.solution: Tier-3 AI Personas were built from real interview data, then used as an always-on panel for concept, interface, and CAD review.
tools.result: Product teams can now pressure-test industrial design decisions immediately instead of waiting on external recruiting cycles.

university.indicator: Household-scale policy simulation moved from years to days.
university.quote: We simulated policy responses across 20,000 households in days. Traditional methods would have taken years.
university.challenge: The research team needed to understand low birth-rate dynamics across family systems that would be impossible to study at physical-panel scale.
university.solution: Researchers interviewed core family members, created AI Personas, and assembled them into household-scale virtual panels for policy simulation.
university.result: They moved from slow qualitative fieldwork to large-scale policy testing in days, while retaining household-level nuance.

prediction.indicator: An agent can call Atypica as a live prediction input layer.
prediction.quote: We call the Subjective World Model through MCP to analyze sentiment on Polymarket events, then let our betting agent act on what the model sees.
prediction.challenge: The system needed a differentiated signal layer for prediction markets, not just more public polling and price data.
prediction.solution: The Subjective World Model was integrated through MCP and wired into an agent-driven prediction stack as a continuous sentiment input.
prediction.result: The agent gained a reusable, machine-readable layer of human interpretation that can directly influence forecasting logic.

storyUI.triggerReach: Trigger reach is still too far in gloves
storyUI.agentInputNote: Agent reads Atypica as a callable subjective signal layer.

closing.title: Ready to Understand Your Users?
closing.body: Let AI model every consumer's subjective world. The harder problem — we've already started.
